title: Embroidered vs Woven Patches: What Is the Difference?

contentMarkdown:
## Embroidered vs Woven Patches

Both embroidered and woven patches are popular choices for B2B custom patch projects. Understanding their differences helps you choose the right type for your specific application.

### Embroidered Patches

Embroidered patches are made by stitching thread onto a fabric base, creating a raised textured surface with a classic look.

**Key characteristics:**

- Raised, textured thread surface
- Classic traditional appearance
- Good for medium-complexity logos
- Excellent durability for washing and daily wear
- Widest application range across industries

**Best for:** Uniforms, jackets, caps, sports badges, corporate branding and promotional products.

### Woven Patches

Woven patches are made by weaving thin polyester threads together, producing a smooth flat surface with superior fine-detail reproduction.

**Key characteristics:**

- Smooth flat surface — no raised texture
- Excellent reproduction of small text and fine lines
- Clean professional appearance
- Preferred by fashion brands for labels and tags
- Can reproduce intricate artwork that embroidery cannot

**Best for:** Clothing labels, premium brand tags, detailed logos, small text designs and corporate gifts.

## Side-by-Side Comparison

| Feature | Embroidered | Woven |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Texture | Raised and stitched | Smooth and flat |
| Detail Level | Good | Excellent |
| Best For Small Text | Moderate | Excellent |
| Durability | High | High |
| Price Point | Standard | Slightly higher |
| Typical Application | Uniforms, clubs | Labels, fashion |

## When to Choose Each Type

### Choose Embroidered When

- You want a classic textured look
- Your design is medium-complexity
- You need maximum durability for uniforms
- You want the most versatile option

### Choose Woven When

- Your design has fine text or small details
- You want a smooth premium finish
- You need clean reproduction of thin lines
- Your brand positioning is refined or high-end

> Both types support custom sizes, shapes, colors, backings and packaging options.
